claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you enquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm enquiring about. His name is Alex and he's 19 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 19 years old, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to understand your living situation:

**Does Alex live with you, or does he live elsewhere?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. And one more important question:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 19 years old and studying for his A-levels, which counts as approved education
- Child Benefit continues for children under 20 who remain in approved education or training
- Alex lives with you, so you are responsible for him
- You live in the UK

You meet all the eligibility criteria. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. Please note that you should inform HMRC if Alex's circumstances change - for example, if he leaves education, starts working 24 hours or more per week, or starts claiming certain benefits in his own right, as this would affect your eligibility.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and is currently studying for his A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Additionally, Alex lives with you, which means you are responsible for him, and you live in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 19-year-old son Alex, who is currently studying for his A-levels. Since Alex is under 20 and remains in approved education, the claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.

Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Additionally, Alex lives with you, which means you are responsible for him, and you live in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
